name: Avenc des Cabrit o des Xinxoram
starting_point: Coll Baix path, km 4.800 (measured from Bar Bodega del Sol).
equipment: Ropes: 1x30 m, 1x10 m. 1 protector. 2 bolts. Oxygen bottle for the CO2 Chamber.
description: A predominantly vertical cave near the Alcúdia cliffs with multiple chambers at different levels. Notable features include the Flag Chamber with beautiful large curtain formations and wall flowstone, and a CO2 Chamber with diverse speleothems but dangerously high carbon dioxide levels (7.35%). A gas detector and oxygen supply are essential for the deepest section, which has a very narrow entrance suitable only for slim cavers.
full_description:
Description

The cavity is located just about fifty meters from the cliff and close to the trail that runs toward the Cova Tancada des Cap de Menorca. A descent of 5 m places us in a small chamber of X x X x 5 m where sparse formations as well as some roots stand out. At one end of the small chamber, a gallery ramp of approximately 6 m in length positions us on a flowstone, marking the beginning of the descent to the Sala de ses Banderoles (Flag Chamber), which is located 10 m below.

Saleta de s'Entrada (Entrance Chamber)

Of reduced size, 1 x 3 x 5 m, the only things noteworthy are some formations and several cupolas. This is one of the two sinks responsible for the development of the cavity.

Sala de ses Banderoles (Flag Chamber)

With a NE-SW orientation, the chamber reaches approximate dimensions of 5.5 x 11 x 10 m. The reconstructive processes in this chamber are abundant, notable among them beautiful curtains of considerable size as well as a wall flowstone that covers the entire side through which one descends. A new widening in the deepest zone of the chamber allowed us to descend to...

Racó dets Ossos (Bones Corner)

A very beautiful corner in the SW zone of the Flag Chamber, which one must climb to reach, conceals the bone remains of a young goat that presumably fell through the chimney, survived the fall, and ended its days taking shelter in this place, hidden behind beautiful formations. At present, rodents have established their residence there.

Sala des CO2 (CO2 Chamber)

With dimensions of 8.30 x 9 x 8.65, and a NW-SE orientation, this is a chamber where constructive processes abound with a great variety of speleothems. Naturally, the name assigned to it corresponds to the high concentration of CO2 it harbors inside — 7.35% on the day the survey of the chamber was conducted.

Note: To visit this cavity, it is essential to always be accompanied by a gas detector. Accessing the CO2 Chamber is very difficult, since in addition to the challenges presented by the access opening to this chamber (suitable only for slim-build cavers), it will be necessary to carry an oxygen bottle due to the high concentration of CO2.
